b: ^Guard: I told you to leave! I'm calling the police!
^[The guard's flashlight beam catches you in the hallway]
^Guard: Hey! What are you doing here? Building's closed for the night.
^Guard: Make it quick. I'm giving you 10 minutes, then you need to be gone.
^Guard: You again. I'm keeping my eye on you.

first_excuse: ^Guard: Well? What's your explanation for being here after hours?
^I work here - forgot something at my desk
^Victoria Sterling asked me to grab some files

c-0: ^You: I work here. I forgot something at my desk earlier.
c-1: ^You: Victoria Sterling asked me to grab some files. I met with her earlier today about the training program.
^Guard: [Pauses] Ms. Sterling mentioned a potential recruit... alright.
c-2: ^You: Building maintenance. Late shift. Checking the HVAC system.
^Guard: Maintenance? I didn't get a work order notice.

excuse_work_here: ^Guard: Huh. I don't recognize you, and I know most of the staff.
^I must have left it at my desk - that's what I came back for

c-0: ^Guard: [Squints at it] That's... that's an executive-level card. Alright, carry on.
^Guard: Just surprised to see someone here this late.
c-1: ^You: I'm new. Just started this week. Still getting my permanent ID.
^Guard: [Skeptical] New hires don't usually have after-hours access...
c-2: ^You: That's what I came back for - my ID badge. Left it at my desk.
^Guard: So you don't have ID, and you're here after hours. That's a problem.

excuse_victoria: ^Guard: Ms. Sterling does sometimes have late requests.
^Guard: What files are you supposed to grab?
^That's confidential - she didn't give me details

c-0: ^You: Training program enrollment documents. From her office.
^Guard: [Nods] Alright. But be quick about it. And stay in the executive area - don't wander.
c-1: ^You: She didn't specify - said I'd know when I saw them. Confidential materials.
^Guard: [Suspicious] Confidential, huh. Well, don't take too long.

excuse_maintenance: ^Guard: No work order, and you don't look like our usual maintenance crew.
^Guard: I'm going to need to verify this.
^Call the maintenance supervisor - here's the number
^Emergency HVAC issue - no time for work orders
^I don't need to explain myself to you

c-0: ^You: Call the supervisor. [Give fake number that could sound plausible]
^Guard: [Looks at number] ...at this hour? Nobody's going to answer.
c-1: ^You: Emergency call. Temperature sensors triggered an alert. No time for paperwork.
^Guard: [Uncertain] I didn't hear about any alerts...
c-2: ^You: I don't have time for this. I have work to do.
^Guard: [Angry] Wrong answer. You're trespassing. Leave now or I'm calling the cops.

suspicious_path: ^Guard: This doesn't add up. You're not making sense.
^Offer a bribe - "Maybe we can work something out"

c-1: ^You: [Elaborate on the lie with more details]
^Guard: [Not buying it] I think you need to leave. Now.

offer_bribe: ^You: Look, maybe we can work something out. I really need to finish something here.
^Guard: [Eyes narrow] Are you trying to bribe me?
^Back off - "No, I just meant maybe you could make an exception"

c-0: ^You: I can make it worth your while. $100. You didn't see me.
c-1: ^You: $500. Cash. Just give me an hour, then I'm gone.
c-2: ^You: No, no - I just meant, could you make an exception? As a favor?
^Guard: [Scoffs] No favors. Leave or I'm calling the police.

bribe_response_low: ^Guard: $100? You think I'm going to risk my job for a hundred bucks?
bribe_response_high: ^Guard: One hour. You finish whatever you're doing and you're gone. I never saw you.
^Guard: And if anyone asks, I was on the other side of the building doing rounds.
^Guard: One hour. After that, you're trespassing and I'm doing my job.
safetynet_reveal: ^You: I'm with SAFETYNET. This is an active investigation into ENTROPY operations.
^This is classified - you can't tell anyone
^Help me and you're a patriot. Hinder me and you're an accomplice.

c-0: ^You: [Show SAFETYNET credentials] I need your cooperation. National security matter.
^Guard: [Stunned] Holy shit. Yeah, okay, whatever you need.
^Guard: Ms. Sterling... she's involved in something?
c-1: ^You: This is classified. You cannot tell anyone I was here. Not even Victoria Sterling.
^Guard: [Nervous] Yeah, understood. I... I won't say anything.
c-2: ^You: Help me, you're helping your country. Get in my way, you're obstructing a federal investigation.
^Guard: [Intimidated] I'm not getting in the way. Do what you need to do.

safetynet_cooperation: ^Guard: What do you need from me?
^That's all I need - continue your patrol

c-0: ^You: Just continue your normal patrol. Pretend you didn't see me.
^Guard: Done. I'll be on the other side of the building if anyone asks.
c-1:
^You: Tell me about Victoria Sterling. What's she like?

^Guard: Ms. Sterling? She's... intense. Smart. Stays late a lot.

^Guard: Sometimes has weird visitors. People who don't look like typical corporate types.

^Guard: But she pays well, so I don't ask questions.
c-2: ^You: Have you noticed anything unusual? Strange visitors? Odd hours?
^Guard: There's been more late-night meetings recently. Last week, some guy with a Russian accent.
^Guard: And Ms. Sterling's been more stressed. Snapping at people.
c-3: ^Guard: Roger that. Good luck with... whatever you're investigating.

b: ^Guard: I'm keeping an eye on you. Don't make me regret this.

ask_shift: ^Guard: Night shift. 10 PM to 6 AM. Quiet most nights.

b: ^Guard: Though tonight's been more eventful than usual.

ask_shift: ^Guard: I do rounds every 15 minutes or so. Check the doors, make sure nobody's where they shouldn't be.

c-0: ^Guard: Main hallway loop. Server room, executive offices, conference area, back to reception.
^Guard: Why do you want to know my route?
c-1: ^Guard: It pays the bills. And it's better than dealing with day shift drama.

ask_building: ^Guard: Standard office building. Reception, conference rooms, main hallway with offices.
^Guard: Server room and IT area in the back. Executive offices on the north side.

b: ^Guard: Server room's usually locked. Executive-level access only.

c-0: ^Guard: Ms. Sterling's office, mostly. Some storage. Conference room for high-level meetings.
c-1: ^Guard: Server room's the main one. And Ms. Sterling doesn't like people in her office without permission.

ask_victoria: ^Guard: Ms. Sterling? She's the boss. CEO. Runs the whole operation.

b:
^Guard: Between you and me, she's a bit intense. Very particular about security protocols.

^Guard: And the people she meets with sometimes... they don't look like normal corporate clients.
^Guard: Why are you asking about Ms. Sterling?

trespass_warning: ^Guard: I'm giving you one chance. Leave now, or I'm calling the police.
hostile_confrontation: ^Guard: That's it. I'm calling the cops. Don't move.
^Tackle the guard before he can call

c-2: ^Guard: [Into radio] Security! I have an intruder!

on_lockpick_detected: ^Guard: HEY! What are you doing with that lock?!
^Guard: You're trying to break in! That's it - I'm calling the police!
on_restricted_area: ^Guard: You're not supposed to be back here. This area is restricted.

b: ^Guard: ...but I guess if Ms. Sterling sent you. Be quick.
^Guard: I need you to return to the main area. Now.
